data.title: Tariffs Are Biggest Risk for Global Economic Outlook, UBS Says — Market Talk
data.description: Tariffs are the biggest risk for the global economic outlook, UBS Chief Economist Arend Kapteyn says in a media briefing. UBS forecasts global growth to slow to 2.9% this year from about 3.2% in 2024, mainly due to uncertainties surrounding the U.S. and Chinese economies. Should Trump go ahead with tariff policies that he campaigned on, such as a 60% tariff on China and 10% tariff on the world, it could bring the "level of U.S. trade restrictiveness back to levels we haven't seen since the mid-1930s," Kapteyn adds. "If we do get those tariffs, there's lots of additional downside risk potentially to our forecast," he adds. However, Kapteyn is skeptical that global tariffs would go ahead, as it would be legally tricky. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Peptonic Medical Secures US Patent for Vulvodynia Relief Gel
data.description:
Peptonic Medical (PMED.ST) said Thursday it secured a US patent for its VagiVital Intimate Calming Gel, a self-care product for women's intimate health.

The patent valid until April 2041 clears the way for the product's launch in the US market. VagiVital is based on the Swedish biomedical company's AktivGel formulation and aims to improve the quality of women's intimate health and provide relief from vulvodynia pain.

data.title: UK CHANCELLOR TO THE EXCHEQUER RACHEL REEVES DOES NOT APPEAR IN PARLIAMENT TO ANSWER URGENT OPPOSITION QUESTION ON RISING GILT YIELDS
U.S. Crude Oil Stocks Fall 1 Million Barrels in Week Ended Jan. 3, EIA Reports
data.description:
U.S. crude oil inventories fell 1 million barrels to 414.6 million barrels in the week ended Jan. 3, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its This Week in Petroleum report released Wednesday.

Crude oil production edged 17,000 barrels per day (b/d) lower to 13.58 million b/d week-over-week, the EIA reported.

Crude oil imports grew by 111,000 b/d to 6.62 million b/d while crude oil exports held relatively steady, only lower by 6,000 b/d from the previous week at 3.89 million b/d, according to the EIA.

Total gasoline stocks jumped 6.3 million barrels to 237.7 million barrels. Distillate stockpiles also increased by 6 million barrels to 128.9 million barrels, the EIA reported.

data.title: WeRide Expands European Presence with Autonomous Robobus Shuttle Deployment at Zurich Airport
data.description:
ZURICH, Jan. 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — WeRide Inc. WRD, a global leader in autonomous driving technology, today announced deployment of its Robobus autonomous shuttle service at Zurich Airport in partnership with Flughafen Zürich AG and Swiss Transit Lab (STL), marking a significant milestone in bringing autonomous mobility solutions to a major European aviation hub, and building on WeRide's successful European operations, including its recent autonomous shuttle service at Roland-Garros during the 2024 French Open.

The WeRide Robobus, which accommodates nine passengers, will be the first commercial autonomous bus shuttle project at a European airport. It will serve airport employees along a dedicated route connecting the employee entrance at gate 101 to the maintenance area at gate 130. Service will begin in the first quarter of 2025.

Flughafen Zürich AG is working with airports in Brussels and Amsterdam that are also testing technologies for autonomous vehicles. Before the start of test Robobus operations, WeRide's autonomous taxi, Robotaxi, completed route mapping at Zurich Airport, demonstrating that WeRide's Robotaxi will have more applications in Switzerland.

At the foot of the Alps, Zurich Airport presents severe challenges to autonomous driving technology due to complex operational environment and climatic conditions. WeRide successfully overcame these challenges with its strong technical capabilities and rich implementation experience, providing a mature and reliable solution.

The Zurich Airport service follows the successful deployment in Paris, where Robobus provided efficient shuttle services on a five-kilometer route during the French Open, as part of an ongoing collaboration with Renault Group to promote low-carbon public transportation solutions.

The Robobus deployment leverages WeRide's advanced autonomous driving technology, which has undergone extensive testing and validation across more than 30 cities in seven countries. The initial phase at Zurich Airport includes a safety driver on board, with plans to transition to remote monitoring operations.

“WeRide possesses a fully developed and thoroughly tested technology, meets all of the legal and safety requirements in place at Zurich Airport and guarantees that it will meet the data protection requirements set out,” Flughafen Zürich AG said in a statement.

The Company will work with Swiss Transit Lab to ensure smooth operation of the service.

Contact

Edmond Lococo

ICR, LLC

WeRidePR@icrinc.com

+1 (332) 242-4398

data.title: Tata Consultancy Services Declares Dividend of INR76 a Share, Including Special Dividend of INR66>532540.BY
India's TCS misses Q3 revenue estimate
data.description:
Tata Consultancy Services TCS, India's largest software company, posted a smaller-than-expected third-quarter revenue on Thursday as clients pared tech spends on account of macro-economic uncertainty.

The company's consolidated revenue increased 5.6% to 639.73 billion rupees ($7.45 billion) in the October-December quarter, falling short of analysts' expectation of 644.52 billion rupees, as per data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 85.8420 Indian rupees)

data.title: COMMENT-FX options reflect the gravity of GBP's vulnerability
data.description:
Massive demand and volumes for GBP-related FX options over the last 24 hours are a reflection of GBP losses and the gravity of the situation in which it finds itself.

Implied volatility gauges actual/realised volatility expectations and is a key part of an option premium. GBP/USD implied volatility has surged to new 2-year highs across its entire 1-12-month expiry term structure. The benchmark 1-month expiry implied volatility jumping another 1.0 to test 11.0 in early London Thursday - a massive 2.5 increase in just 24 hours.

GBP put demand - options giving the right to sell GBP, has also surged as shown by risk reversal contracts. In the last 24 hours, the GBP put over GBP call implied volatility premium has tripled to 1.5 in the benchmark 1-month expiry 25 delta contract and is significantly higher across the entire term structure.

DTCC traded options data shows that volumes of GBP related options over the last 24 hours are close to those achieved when GBP hit a record low after the infamous Truss budget in 2022. DTCC data also shows traders buying an array of GBP put strikes between 1.2000 and 1.1500, which would cover the risk of GBP plummeting toward those levels and taking option implied volatility ever higher.

Additional near term risks for GBP/USD are a speech by the Deputy Bank of England Governor late Thursday and Friday's NFP data, which have doubled the price of overnight expiry volatility premiums.

For more click on

data.title: TREASURY SECRETARY SENDS CHIEF SECRETARY DARREN JONES TO ANSWER URGENT PARLIAMENTARY QUESTIONS ON GILT YIELDS
Repsol Shares to Rise on Earnings Momentum and Share Buybacks — Market Talk
data.description: Repsol shares look set to rise as refining margins rebound, UBS analysts write. The Spanish energy major's stock has fallen 12% in the past six months and investors fail to account for higher gas prices and Repsol's earnings momentum, the analysts write. The analysts increase their earnings per share forecasts by 17%, putting them 8% ahead of consensus expectations. Repsol shares could be boosted by buybacks worth around 800 million euros in 2025, a UBS forecast that is nearly 150 million euros ahead of consensus expectations. A strong balance sheet and asset sales across should also boost shares, they write. UBS upgrades the stock's rating to buy from neutral, and increases its target price to 13 euros from 11.5 euros. Shares trade up 2.1% at 12.04 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: Zephyr Energy Says Rig Mobilized to US Well Site, Expects Drilling to Start in Mid-January
data.description:
Zephyr Energy ZPHR said Thursday that a rig is being mobilized to drill an extended lateral on the State 36-2 LNW-CC-R well at its Paradox project in Utah, US.

The rigging process involving Nabors Drilling USA's B29 rig is expected to be completed shortly, with drilling operations on track to begin in mid-January, according to the oil and gas company. Once the well has spudded, drilling is seen taking 30 days.

data.title: European Midday Briefing: Surge in Gilt Yields Continues, Raising Concern, as Sterling Falls
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Stocks:

European indices were mixed, with the FTSE 100 posting solid gains after sterling hit a one-year low against the dollar following a sharp selloff in U.K. government bonds.

Elsewhere European shipping companies were under pressure after a strike was averted at major U.S. ports, with Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd both down more than 5%.

Jefferies said freight rates have been boosted recently as retailers increase their stockpile of inventory amid uncertainty surrounding tariffs and the potential port strikes.

"The supply/demand balance remains steady, but a soft patch could come later this year."

Stocks to Watch

The impact of lower interest rates sees a continuation of the challenging operating environment for Nordic banks, Jefferies said separately, as it lowered its EPS estimates by 1%-3% on average across the sector.

"On the macro front, economic sentiment remains weak, and we await an economic recovery in Sweden."

Nordea remains its only buy-rated stock among the Nordic banks, as it continues to see better value elsewhere in the European banking context.

U.S. Markets:

The U.S. stock market will be closed and the bond market will shut early, but Thursday will bring a plethora of timely commentary from Federal Reserve officials as the central bank faces an uncertain inflation outlook.

The Fed's Collins and Harker will both speak around 14:00 GMT, with Barkin, Bowman and Schmid delivering remarks at events later in the day.

The next big impact on rate expectations will be Friday's nonfarm payrolls data.

Forex:

The dollar is likely to remain firm after the Fed minutes confirmed its more cautious stance about cutting interest rates, ING said, adding that the currency is likely to stay strong into Trump's inauguration on January 20.

Sterling could extend its losses--falling below $1.2--on growing investor unease over the ongoing selloff in gilts, MUFG said.

The rise in gilt yields appears broadly in line with similar moves in other bond markets, but rising borrowing costs could put pressure on the government to tighten fiscal policy.

Higher yields due to the Bank of England's caution over cutting interest rates boosted sterling last year.

"Recent price action highlights though, that higher yields are not always positive for a currency if they are driven by unease over the public finances and inflation in the U.K."

Pepperstone said a falling currency and that rising yields were a classic sign of fiscal de-anchoring and of participants losing confidence in the government's ability to exert control over the fiscal backdrop.

It added that it wasn't the same situation as when the 2022 tax-slashing budget under former Prime Minister Liz Truss sparked market turmoil but "things are clearly on very shaky ground" and selling sterling remains its preference.

UOB said the pound is likely to continue its downtrend against the dollar, in coming weeks according to the weekly chart .

Bonds:

Issuance in the eurozone so far has been strongly received, but bond yields keep rising.

"For most of December it was U.S. Treasuries that led the way, since the holidays it has been eurozone government bonds and since yesterday it is U.K. gilts," Commerzbank said.

The rise in Treasury yields in recent weeks has been driven by rising inflation expectations, the repricing of the Fed's interest rates and higher term premium, Metzler said.

"While a rise in the term premium is likely to continue (political uncertainty is likely to remain high under Trump), the mood regarding the Fed, the economy and inflation could quickly change again."

The first signs of that came Wednesday when the Fed's Waller said he expected milder inflation and more rate cuts.

Gilt yields continued to rise--more than other bond yields--and if the current moves continue, it could potentially be evidence of a buyer's strike or capital flight, according to Fidelity.

Energy:

Oil was lower due to a stronger dollar and rise in U.S. fuel stockpiles.

"From a technical perspective, crude oil [WTI] is now in the medium-term bullish consolidation zone and should remain there above the $72.85pb support," Swissquote Bank said.

Prices had a strong start to 2025, supported by colder temperatures boosting heating fuel consumption and worries over Iranian and Russian supplies.

Still, broader concerns of an oversupplied market this year and uncertainties over Chinese growth limit the upside.

Metals:

Gold edged higher despite a stronger dollar, as investors seek safe-haven assets amid growing market uncertainties.

A weaker-than-expected report on U.S. private employment for December provided some reassurance that the Fed might be less cautious about lowering rates.

But the minutes released on Wednesday suggested officials will hold rates steady for now, revealing they saw risks of higher-than-expected inflation partly due to Trump's potential future policies.

"That should hardly come as a surprise given that the median interest rate projection now shows only 50bp in cuts this year," Capital Economics said.

Global Lithium Outlook

Mergers and acquisitions are likely to remain prevalent in the global lithium industry , as valuations decline on expectations for lower lithium prices in the medium term, Goldman Sachs said.

It said the lithium market should be soft again this year as a number of projects ramp up, and reckon producers will be focused on cost-cutting and production efficiency.

EMEA HEADLINES

German Industrial Production Rises Despite Fears of Layoffs, Tariffs

German industrial production ticked up in November last year, offering a glimmer of hope for the beleaguered sector in the face of sweeping job cuts and the threat of import tariffs from the incoming Trump administration.

Output rose 1.5% on month in November, German statistics agency Destatis said Thursday, better than the 0.5% rise expected by economists in a poll compiled by The Wall Street Journal.

China Blasts EU's Foreign Subsidies Law as Unfair Trade Barrier

Beijing has called out the European Union for imposing what it says are unfair trade barriers on Chinese companies, ending a monthslong investigation into the bloc's efforts to protect its businesses from foreign subsidies.

China's commerce ministry released the results of its probe into the EU's Foreign Subsidies Regulation on Thursday, saying that European authorities have implemented the rule selectively in a way that infringes on Chinese companies' interests.

Tesco Sticks to Profit Target After Posting Christmas Sales Growth

Tesco reiterated its profit target for its fiscal year after reporting sales growth during the Christmas period.

The U.K. supermarket chain on Thursday said it continues to expect retail adjusted operating profit-its preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items-in the fiscal year ending in February of around 2.9 billion pounds ($3.59 billion).

Marks & Spencer Focused on Growth Plan After Christmas Sales Boost Results

Marks & Spencer said it is confident in making progress in the remainder of the year after it recorded a positive performance during the key Christmas season and as it continues with its growth plan.

The U.K. retailer said Thursday that for the 13 weeks to Dec. 28 it made sales of 4.06 billion pounds ($5.02 billion), up 5.6% compared with the year-earlier period. The result came ahead of analysts' projections of 3.96 billion pounds, according to a Visible Alpha poll of estimates.

GLOBAL NEWS

Trump's Day 1: Incoming president looks set to act on tariffs, crypto, energy and immigration

On his first day back in office, President-elect Donald Trump is planning to go big on executive orders and other actions, including ones that aim to roll back some of his predecessor's policies. It'll be similar to how President Joe Biden rolled out a raft of orders on the day of his inauguration four years ago and targeted a number of Trump 1.0 initiatives.

So which upcoming Trump executive actions on Jan. 20 could have an effect on your investing portfolio or your wallet? Here's a brief guide.

Musk Says DOGE's Goal to Cut $2 Trillion in Spending Is 'Best-Case Outcome'

Elon Musk reiterated that his Department of Government Efficiency will try to cut $2 trillion from federal spending, but he said they may not reach that goal.

"I think if we try for $2 trillion, we've got a good shot at getting one, " Musk said Wednesday in a conversation with Mark Penn, chairman and chief executive of ad company Stagwell, that was livestreamed on Musk's social-media platform X.

Trump's NATO Vision Spells Trouble for the Alliance

President-elect Donald Trump's latest demands of America's NATO partners-that they cede territory to the U.S. and spend more on defense than Washington itself does-risk undermining allies' confidence and potentially emboldening adversaries.

In a press conference Tuesday, Trump raised the prospect of forcibly taking over Canada and Greenland, which is part of Denmark. Canada and Denmark are founding members of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, and the U.S. is treaty-bound to protect them.

Israel's Defense Industry Booms on Foreign Demand and War at Home

TEL AVIV-After a year marked by war and calls for isolating Israel on the world stage, its defense industry is on track to sell more weapons than ever in its history, spurred by foreign demand for its aerial-defense systems and significant government spending aimed at funding Israel's ongoing military campaigns.

The surge in weapons sales for Israel's top three defense companies-among the world's top 10 defense exporters-comes amid widespread arms embargoes on Israel, from even some of its allies. Other countries have suspended their acquisition of Israeli arms over the war in Gaza.

Los Angeles Wildfires Leave at Least Five Dead as Uncontained Blazes Intensify LOS ANGELES-Portions of the heavily populated Hollywood neighborhood were ordered to evacuate Wednesday evening as a new fire broke out in the hills above the center of this city battling numerous deadly blazes.

At least five people were dead and tens of thousands were forced to evacuate as six wildfires simultaneously hit the most populated county in America. More than 2,000 structures were damaged or destroyed, 123,000 people lost power, and the region's firefighting resources were stretched to their limits.

Write to clare.kinloch@wsj.com

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Canadian Natural Resources forecasts higher production in 2025
data.description:
Canadian Natural Resources CNQ said on Tuesday it expects production to increase 12% in 2025, betting on higher demand amid tight supplies.

The company said annual average production in 2025 is targeted to be between 1.51 million and 1.555 million barrels of oil equivalent per day (boepd), resulting in production growth of approximately 170,000 boepd or 12% over 2024 levels based on the mid-point of forecast.

data.title: Acer Announces Preliminary Full Year 2024 Revenues at NT$264.71B, Up 9.7% >2353.TW
Air Products and Chemicals Pours Cold Water on Activist Investor Mantle Ridge's CEO, Chair Nominations
data.description:
Air Products and Chemicals APD said late Wednesday that its shareholder Mantle Ridge's proposed nominations of candidates for the chief executive officer and chair roles "should be troubling" to stockholders.

The company's statement is its board's response to activist investor Mantle Ridge's shareholder letter introducing four director nominees, including Executive Chair Dennis Reilley and its proposed CEO candidate, Eduardo Menezes.

Reilley has not held a management position in 18 years and would be coming into a company and industry that have undergone tremendous change in the last two decades, rendering any "experience" that he has as stale, Air Products said.

"Menezes, who has never been a public company CEO, only had responsibility for one region when he worked at Linde, and, as acknowledged by Mantle Ridge, was passed over for CEO at Linde," Air Products added.

Mantle Ridge did not immediately respond to MTNewswires' request for comment early on Thursday.

Air Products' shares slipped 0.4% at the close on Wednesday.

In its shareholder letter, Mantle Ridge urged investors to vote for its nominees "to enhance Air Products' performance and create the long-term value that shareholders deserve." Mantle Ridge said it beneficially owns $1.3 billion of Air Products' outstanding common shares.

data.title: Nordic forward prices fall on wetter weather view, higher water reserves
data.description:
Nordic forward power prices fell on Thursday, as forecasts for the current week showed wetter and warmer weather in the hydropower-reliant region, with higher water reserves also adding pressure.

* Nordic front-quarter baseload power contract (ENOFBLQc1) fell 0.75 euros to 17.3 euros per megawatt hour (MWh) at 1015 GMT.

* Nordic front-year (ENOFBLYc1) lost 0.3 euros to 32.5 euros per MWh.

* "The downtrend could very well continue today where the forecasts are even wetter and milder than yesterday," analysts at Energi Danmark said in a note.

* "The hydro balance surplus should grow if the forecasts turn out to be true and the outlook is therefore still bearish for the Nordic power market."

* Nordic water reserves available 15 days ahead (PCAEC00) were seen at 23.84 terawatt hours (TWh) above normal, compared with 22.87 TWh above normal on Wednesday.

* In the European carbon market, the benchmark contract (CFI2Zc1) was down 0.22 euro at 71.9 euros a metric ton.

* German year-ahead power (TRDEBYc1) slipped 0.3% to 89.1 euros per MWh.

* European prompt power prices were mixed with increased wind supply weighing on the German price, while the French price gained as temperatures were seen falling in the country, increasing demand expectations.

* "The northeast of Central Europe will continue to be partially cloudy with occasional light precipitation mostly as rain," Georg Muller, meteorologist at LSEG, said in a note.

* However, "next week will be generally calm and dry in most areas."

* The Nordic power price for next-day physical delivery (FXSYSAL=NPX), or system price, rose 47.75 euros to 96.88 euros per MWh.

data.title: LG Electronics Forecasts Record 2024 Revenue
data.description:
LG Electronics 066570 forecasts a consolidated revenue of 87.74 trillion won in 2024, marking a new record for the highest annual revenue in the firm's history.

The South Korean electronics products maker also expects its annual operating profit to hit 3.43 trillion won, according to a Wednesday news release.

Over the past four years, LG's revenue grew at a compound annual rate of over 10%, driven by strategic shifts such as subscription services, direct-to-consumer initiatives, and expanded business-to-business operations.

Despite challenges, profitability stayed stable, with positive growth fueled by portfolio realignment, though shipping costs and inventory adjustments impacted second-half profits, the release said.

Shares of LG Electronics fell over 1% at market close on Thursday.

data.title: JBT Marel Corporation: Major shareholder announcement - Eyrir Invest hf.
data.description: Attached is a major shareholder announcement from Eyrir Invest hf., in English and Icelandic.

data.title: Ajmera Realty Clocks 7% Increase in Fiscal Q3 Sales Value
data.description:
Ajmera Realty & Infra India AJMERA recorded sales value of 2.70 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter, up 7% from the year-ago period, according to a press release filed with the Indian bourses on Thursday.

Collections during the quarter ended Dec. 31 rose 10% year on year to 1.67 billion rupees, the release said.

data.title: Alfa Laval to Open Food Innovation Center in Denmark
data.description:
Alfa Laval ALFA said Thursday it will establish a food innovation center at the headquarters of its food and water business in Copenhagen, Denmark.

The 1,200-square-meter facility will serve as a research and development hub to help food producers address the challenges of expanding production while minimizing environmental impact.

Works have already started and the facility is scheduled to open in 2027, according to the Swedish heat transfer, separation and fluid handling products group.

data.description: NVIDIA NVDA has an average rating of buy and mean price target of $174.60, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Nexstim to Deliver Navigated Brain Stimulation System to US Healthcare
data.description:
Nexstim NXTMH secured an order to provide its Navigated Brain Stimulation System 5 with its NexSpeech module to a healthcare provider in the US state of Ohio.

The Finnish medtech company's combined technology will be used by the customer for the preprocedural mapping of the motor cortex of the brain and speech areas, evaluation of surgical risks and neuromonitoring, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: S&P Assigns BBB+ Rating to AmBank's Proposed US Dollar Bonds
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings has placed a BBB+ long-term issue rating on AmBank's proposed US dollar senior unsecured bonds, according to a Thursday release.

The bonds' rating is in line with the Malaysia-based bank's issuer credit rating, reflecting S&P's view that the assets will rank equally with the bank's other obligations.

The bank will channel the proceeds for general working capital purposes.

AmBank is a subsidiary of AMMB Holdings Berhad AMBANK.

data.title: Cosco Capital Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
Cosco Capital COSCO bought back 200,000 shares at between 5.53 Philippine pesos and 5.55 pesos apiece, according to a Wednesday Philippine bourse disclosure.

The retail holding company has so far spent 2.03 billion pesos out of the 4 billion pesos allocated under its buyback program.
